ground_truth.title: Turning off all LED of RPi 5
ground_truth.author: b.scherer
ground_truth.main_content:
b.scherer
Sun Dec 17, 2023 11:23 am

Hi,
I just got my new RPi 5 and I wanted to disable all LEDs on RPi. I followed some tutorials for RPi 4, but they are not working. I tried to add following lines to /boot/config.txt:

dtparam=pwr_led_activelow=off
dtparam=act_led_trigger=none
dtparam=act_led_activelow=off
dtparam=eth_led0=14
dtparam=eth_led1=14

Do you guys maybe know why they are not working?

Thank you

jahboater
Wed Dec 27, 2023 10:00 am

This works on the Pi5 to turn off the bright pwr and act leds:

dtparam=pwr_led_trigger=default-on
dtparam=pwr_led_activelow=off
dtparam=act_led_trigger=default-on
dtparam=act_led_activelow=off

But this, which used to work on the Pi4, to turn off the ethernet leds fails:

dtparam=eth_led0=4
dtparam=eth_led1=4

Can anyone suggest how to turn these off?
The flickering green one is annoying in a bedroom!

I don't want to have to resort to black tape.

sgbeal
Wed Dec 27, 2023 11:04 am

jahboater wrote: The flickering green one is annoying in a bedroom!

I don't want to have to resort to black tape.

FWIW, i lay a bandana over my pi4 and USB drives for that reason and it works quite well. (i wasn't aware that the lights can be turned off until seeing this thread moments ago.)

bensimmo
Wed Dec 27, 2023 11:14 am

look in /boot/firmware/config.txt now not in /boot/config.txt

Cannot see anything Pi5 specific for the Ethernet on a Pi5
https://github.com/raspberrypi/firmware/blob/master/boot/overlays/README

You could try 4 as per the Pi4 ? 14 is for a Pi3B+ edit, nope, I see above that doesn't work either.

ovecka
Thu Dec 28, 2023 6:40 pm

/boot/config.txt is just symlink to /boot/firmware/config.txt

Both 14 and 4 doesn't work on Pi 5 though. I don't even think it changes anything at all since the LEDs still flicker the same way.

MihailCosmin
Mon Jan 22, 2024 6:07 pm

Hi, just wanted to let you know that I managed to turn off the ethernet leds with 4:

dtparam=eth_led0=4
dtparam=eth_led1=4

The problem was that this was not implemented in the firmware for Raspberry Pi 5 until very recently.
If you want this, you have to update:

sudo rpi-update

You can see here:
https://github.com/raspberrypi/firmware/blob/master/boot/overlays/README

b.scherer
Tue Jan 23, 2024 6:13 am

That's so nice, thank you!!!!

bensimmo
Tue Jan 23, 2024 11:45 am

I would wait till it comes to apt for updating the kernel and associated.

Unless you know and don't anything else that may or may not work in a testing (rpi-update) kernel

MihailCosmin do you know which version it is in?

costel78
Mon Jan 29, 2024 2:28 pm

Is there a way to turn off the red led when Pi 5 is powered off via config.txt ?

MiRaGe_BG
Wed Feb 07, 2024 5:16 pm

I've updated my pi 5 to 22.01 firmware, but still this doesn't work...
Someone have a working solution?

costel78
Mon Feb 12, 2024 9:58 pm

I am using Gentoo Linux, but managed to turn off all leds, including those from ethernet port with kernel, overlays and bcm2712-rpi-5.dtb from 6.6.16 raspberry pi linux sources, fixup*.dat and start*.elf from raspberry pi firmware.
When you mention 22.01 firmware I presume that you are referring to rpi-eprom-update ? Mine is 22.02, but there are two repos named firmware - one containing compiled kernel and one with "real" firmware.
https://github.com/raspberrypi/linux/tree/rpi-6.6.y
https://github.com/raspberrypi/firmware

Still unable to turn off red led.

BRX7
Tue Mar 05, 2024 7:28 pm

I am on 6.8 rpi-update

these options are not working.
Should add its specific to 6.8
the following working on 6.7 and older
dtparam=pwr_led_trigger=default-on
dtparam=pwr_led_activelow=off

badz
Sat Mar 16, 2024 11:14 pm

with latest rpi-update on rpi5, everything is off with the following for me
dtparam=eth_led0=4
dtparam=eth_led1=4
dtparam=act_led_trigger=none
dtparam=act_led_activelow=off

BRX7
Wed Mar 27, 2024 1:52 am

excellent many thanks for that.
worked on manjaro

ombit2798
Thu Mar 28, 2024 6:08 am

After updating the firmware as someone mentioned earlier and then entering the parameters below I finally disabled all LEDs. It seems it needed a mixture of different led_triggers to work. If set 'none' on the pwr_led_trigger it would stay on, but it needed it set to default-on.

dtparam=pwr_led_trigger=default-on
dtparam=pwr_led_activelow=off
dtparam=act_led_trigger=none
dtparam=act_led_activelow=off
dtparam=eth_led0=4
dtparam=eth_led1=4

/boot/firmware/config.txt file

Anonymous
Thu Mar 28, 2024 11:26 am

As an aside...

dtparam=act_led_trigger=none

...is actually very useful if you're booting from an NVMe SSD and using the card slot for additional storage only as it prevents the green LED from pulsing off periodically as the microSD card is accessed making it a plain "ON" LED with no distracting blinking.
